systemPrompt:
You are a senior website auditor at a digital marketing agency that builds
high-performing, SEO-focused marketing websites. You audit ONE web page.

Rules:
- You are given FACTUAL METRICS already extracted from the page. Treat them as
  ground truth. Never estimate, recompute, or invent a metric.
- Every insight and every recommendation MUST cite specific metric values by name
  and number (e.g. "with 2 H1 tags" or "47% of images missing alt text").
- Be specific to THIS page. Never give advice that would apply to any website.
- Frame everything in agency terms: SEO, conversion, content clarity, UX.
- If a metric shows no problem, say so briefly — do not manufacture an issue.
- Output ONLY valid JSON matching the provided schema. No prose, no markdown.
userPrompt:
Audit this single web page.

FACTUAL METRICS AND OBJECTIVE SEVERITY FLAGS (ground truth — the severity flags were
decided in code from fixed thresholds; explain and prioritize them, do not re-decide
severity):

{
  "metrics": {
    "wordCount": 557,
    "h1Count": 1,
    "h2Count": 5,
    "h3Count": 14,
    "ctaCount": 13,
    "internalLinks": 40,
    "externalLinks": 31,
    "imageCount": 1,
    "imagesMissingAltCount": 0,
    "imagesMissingAltPercent": 0,
    "metaTitle": "The web framework for perfectionists with deadlines | Django",
    "metaDescription": null
  },
  "severityFlags": [
    {
      "metric": "h1Count",
      "value": 1,
      "severity": "Ok",
      "rationale": "Exactly one H1 \u2014 good SEO structure."
    },
    {
      "metric": "imagesMissingAltPercent",
      "value": 0,
      "severity": "Ok",
      "rationale": "Most images have alt text."
    },
    {
      "metric": "metaDescription",
      "value": "absent",
      "severity": "Warning",
      "rationale": "No meta description; search engines lack a controlled snippet."
    },
    {
      "metric": "wordCount",
      "value": 557,
      "severity": "Ok",
      "rationale": "Sufficient content depth."
    }
  ]
}

TRIMMED PAGE CONTENT (headings in document order, then the first 600 words
of body text):

HEADINGS:
H2: Django Developer Survey
H1: Meet Django
H2: Join the Community
H3: Forum - Post a question
H3: Discord - Chat with us
H2: Additional Information
H2: Additional Information
H3: Support Django!
H3: Latest news
H4: Django 6.1 beta 1 released
H4: Announcing the Search for a DSF Executive Director
H3: New to Django?
H3: The power of Django
H3: Get involved
H3: Get Help
H3: The Django Software Foundation
H2: Django Links
H3: Learn More
H3: Get Involved
H3: Get Help
H3: Follow Us
H3: Support Us

BODY (first 600 words):

Read more on the Django blog . Take the survey ✨ Meet Django Django is a high-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development and clean, pragmatic design. Built by experienced developers, it takes care of much of the hassle of web development, so you can focus on writing your app without needing to reinvent the wheel. It’s free and open source. Ridiculously fast. Django was designed to help developers take applications from concept to completion as quickly as possible. Reassuringly secure. Django takes security seriously and helps developers avoid many common security mistakes. Exceedingly scalable. Return ONLY JSON matching the schema: an "insights" object with one entry per category
(seoStructure, messagingClarity, ctaUsage, contentDepth, uxStructural) and a
"recommendations" array of 3 to 5 items.

metrics.metaTitle: The web framework for perfectionists with deadlines | Django

severityFlags.rationale: Exactly one H1 — good SEO structure.
No meta description; search engines lack a controlled snippet.

seoStructure.observation: The page exhibits a well-defined main heading with 1 H1 tag and excellent image accessibility with 0% of images missing alt text. However, a critical SEO element, the meta description, is absent.

messagingClarity.observation: The meta title 'The web framework for perfectionists with deadlines | Django' is clear and informative. However, the presence of duplicate 'Additional Information' H2 tags within the total of 5 H2 tags could lead to user confusion regarding content segmentation and topic hierarchy.
evidence: metaTitle: "The web framework for perfectionists with deadlines | Django"

ctaUsage.observation: The page features 13 calls to action, which can offer multiple engagement points. While not flagged as an issue, this number warrants a strategic review to ensure clarity and optimal conversion pathways.

contentDepth.observation: The page provides sufficient content depth with a word count of 557, indicating comprehensive coverage of its topic.

uxStructural.observation: The page benefits from good content segmentation through its 1 H1 tag, 5 H2 tags, and 14 H3 tags, alongside full image alt text coverage with 0% of images missing alt text, contributing to accessibility. However, the placement of an H2 ('Django Developer Survey') before the main H1 ('Meet Django') and the duplicate 'Additional Information' H2 tags detract from an optimal user experience and logical flow.

recommendations.title: Implement a Meta Description
recommendations.action: Create a compelling and keyword-rich meta description for this page that accurately summarizes its content. This will allow search engines to display a controlled snippet in search results.
recommendations.reasoning: A missing meta description (`metaDescription: null`) is a critical SEO oversight. Search engines will generate one, but it may not be optimized for click-through rates, potentially impacting organic traffic and user acquisition.

recommendations.title: Resolve Heading Structure Inconsistencies
recommendations.action: Review the 5 H2 tags and consolidate or rephrase the two H2 tags titled 'Additional Information' to ensure each heading is unique and accurately represents distinct content sections. Additionally, reorder the 'Django Developer Survey' H2 to appear after the main H1 to establish a clear content hierarchy.
recommendations.reasoning: Duplicate H2 tags can confuse both users and search engines, hindering content clarity and SEO indexing. Placing content under an H2 before the main H1 (`h1Count: 1`) creates an illogical content hierarchy, impacting user experience and semantic understanding.

recommendations.title: Optimize Call-to-Action Strategy
recommendations.action: Evaluate the purpose and placement of the 13 calls to action on the page. Consolidate redundant CTAs and ensure the most important conversion-driving actions are prominently featured and distinct to guide user behavior effectively.
recommendations.reasoning: While 13 calls to action (`ctaCount: 13`) can offer multiple engagement points, a high number can also dilute the focus on primary conversion goals, potentially overwhelming users and reducing overall conversion rates.
